news.title:
Motilal Oswal upgrades Cera Sanitaryware to 'Buy', citing 28% upside based on 25x FY28E P/E ratio.
Cera projects 11% CAGR revenue, Rs 1,000 crore cash surplus by FY28; stock attractive post 15% correction.
news.summary:
Motilal Oswal upgrades Cera Sanitaryware to 'Buy', citing 28% upside based on 25x FY28E P/E ratio.
Cera projects 11% CAGR revenue, Rs 1,000 crore cash surplus by FY28; stock attractive post 15% correction.

news.title:
Cera Sanitaryware faces Rs. 44.6 crore tax demand for FY20, including GST shortfall.
Company claims no material financial impact and plans to appeal the decision.
news.summary:
Cera Sanitaryware faces Rs. 44.6 crore tax demand for FY20, including GST shortfall.
Company claims no material financial impact and plans to appeal the decision.

news.title:
Geopolitical tensions and rising crude prices impact consumer durables, plywood, pipes, and tiles sectors, causing cost inflation and margin concerns.
Amber Enterprises, Voltas, Blue Star, Havells, Greenply, Century Plyboards, Kajaria Ceramics, Cera Sanitaryware, Finolex, Astral, and Prince Pipes see stock declines since Feb 27.
news.summary:
Geopolitical tensions and rising crude prices impact consumer durables, plywood, pipes, and tiles sectors, causing cost inflation and margin concerns.
Amber Enterprises, Voltas, Blue Star, Havells, Greenply, Century Plyboards, Kajaria Ceramics, Cera Sanitaryware, Finolex, Astral, and Prince Pipes see stock declines since Feb 27.

news.title:
Cera Sanitaryware Ltd faces a 50% gas supply cut from Sabarmati Gas due to West Asian crisis.
Despite the cut, production impact is temporary as GAIL provides an alternative supply.
news.summary:
Cera Sanitaryware Ltd faces a 50% gas supply cut from Sabarmati Gas due to West Asian crisis.
Despite the cut, production impact is temporary as GAIL provides an alternative supply.

news.title:
Cera Sanitaryware Ltd. shared its investor presentation under SEBI LODR Regulation 30, highlighting its financials, ESG initiatives, and growth strategies.
FY25 revenue reached Rs. 1,915 crore with Rs. 757 crore cash reserves, showcasing robust financials, zero debt, and disciplined capital allocation.
news.summary:
Cera Sanitaryware Ltd. shared its investor presentation under SEBI LODR Regulation 30, highlighting its financials, ESG initiatives, and growth strategies.
FY25 revenue reached Rs. 1,915 crore with Rs. 757 crore cash reserves, showcasing robust financials, zero debt, and disciplined capital allocation.

news.title: Cera Sanitaryware's Q3 net profit fell to ₹237M from ₹459M YoY.
news.summary: Cera Sanitaryware's Q3 net profit fell to ₹237M from ₹459M YoY.

news.title:
Cera Sanitaryware's board meeting on 4th Feb 2026 will review unaudited financial results for Q3 and nine months ending Dec 2025.
Trading window for Cera shares closed on 1st Jan 2026, reopening 48 hours post results release per SEBI regulations.
news.summary:
Cera Sanitaryware's board meeting on 4th Feb 2026 will review unaudited financial results for Q3 and nine months ending Dec 2025.
Trading window for Cera shares closed on 1st Jan 2026, reopening 48 hours post results release per SEBI regulations.

news.title:
Cera Sanitaryware's valuation metrics show shifts in market assessment via P/E and P/B ratios.
These metrics provide a nuanced view of price attractiveness versus historical and peer benchmarks.
news.summary:
Cera Sanitaryware's valuation metrics show shifts in market assessment via P/E and P/B ratios.
These metrics provide a nuanced view of price attractiveness versus historical and peer benchmarks.

news.title:
Cera Sanitaryware Ltd conducted NSE block trade for 35,017 shares at Rs. 5537.5.
The transaction totaled Rs. 19.39 crores, highlighting significant activity.
news.summary:
Cera Sanitaryware Ltd conducted NSE block trade for 35,017 shares at Rs. 5537.5.
The transaction totaled Rs. 19.39 crores, highlighting significant activity.

news.title:
Cera reports Q2 FY26 revenue at INR 488 cr, stable YoY; Sanitaryware up 1.4%, Faucetware down 3.5%.
Project sales form 39% of topline; new brands Senator & Polipluz forecast INR 40-45 cr in H2 FY26.
news.summary:
Cera reports Q2 FY26 revenue at INR 488 cr, stable YoY; Sanitaryware up 1.4%, Faucetware down 3.5%.
Project sales form 39% of topline; new brands Senator & Polipluz forecast INR 40-45 cr in H2 FY26.

news.title: Cera Sanitaryware aims for 7-8% revenue growth by FY26 with 14.5-15% operating margins.
news.summary: Cera Sanitaryware aims for 7-8% revenue growth by FY26 with 14.5-15% operating margins.

news.title:
Cera Sanitaryware conveyed cautious optimism for H2 during their concall.
The company anticipates a stable outlook despite market challenges.
news.summary:
Cera Sanitaryware conveyed cautious optimism for H2 during their concall.
The company anticipates a stable outlook despite market challenges.

news.title:
Cera's Q2 FY26 revenue down 0.4% YoY at Rs. 4879 mn; EBITDA fell 3.6% YoY to Rs. 671 mn.
PAT dropped 16.9% YoY to Rs. 566 mn; focus remains on new brands, dealer system, cost control.
news.summary:
Cera's Q2 FY26 revenue down 0.4% YoY at Rs. 4879 mn; EBITDA fell 3.6% YoY to Rs. 671 mn.
PAT dropped 16.9% YoY to Rs. 566 mn; focus remains on new brands, dealer system, cost control.

news.title:
Cera Sanitaryware's Q2 EBITDA dropped to 671M INR vs 696M YoY.
EBITDA margin fell to 13.8% from 14.2% YoY.
news.summary:
Cera Sanitaryware's Q2 EBITDA dropped to 671M INR vs 696M YoY.
EBITDA margin fell to 13.8% from 14.2% YoY.

news.title:
Cera Sanitaryware Ltd saw a 5.7% decline last week, impacting private companies holding 33% stake.
Top 4 shareholders own 53% of the company, including Vikram Investments and its Co-CEO Vikram Somany.
news.summary:
Cera Sanitaryware Ltd saw a 5.7% decline last week, impacting private companies holding 33% stake.
Top 4 shareholders own 53% of the company, including Vikram Investments and its Co-CEO Vikram Somany.
